metadata.name: ISO 22301:2019 Business Continuity Management System
metadata.description: ISO 22301:2019 specifies requirements to plan, establish, implement, operate, monitor, review, maintain, and continually improve a documented business continuity management system (BCMS). It is designed to protect against, reduce the likelihood of, and ensure recovery from disruptive incidents.
metadata.classification_reasoning: ISO 22301 is a certifiable international standard for business continuity management systems. Organizations can achieve certification through accredited certification bodies following a formal audit process.
metadata.framework_purpose: To provide a framework for planning, establishing, implementing, operating, monitoring, reviewing, maintaining, and continually improving a business continuity management system (BCMS) to prepare for, respond to, and recover from disruptions.
metadata.framework_scope: Applicable to all organizations regardless of type, size, or nature, that wish to establish, implement, maintain, and improve a BCMS to ensure conformity with stated business continuity policy.
framework_objectives: Understand the organization's needs and the necessity for establishing business continuity management policy and objectives
Implement and operate controls and measures for managing an organization's overall ability to manage disruptive incidents
Monitor and review the performance and effectiveness of the BCMS
Continually improve based on objective measurement
metadata.target_audience: Business Continuity Managers, Risk Managers, IT Disaster Recovery Teams, Operations Managers, Senior Leadership, Compliance Officers, and Internal Auditors
metadata.certification_body: Accredited Certification Bodies (e.g., BSI, DNV, Bureau Veritas, TÜV, Lloyd's Register)
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Loss of certification, reputational damage, inability to demonstrate resilience to stakeholders, potential contractual non-compliance with partners requiring ISO 22301 certification.
adoption_approach: Define BCMS scope and obtain leadership commitment
Conduct Business Impact Analysis (BIA) and Risk Assessment
Develop business continuity strategies and solutions
Create and document business continuity plans and procedures
Conduct internal audits and management reviews
Undergo certification audit by accredited body

controls.title: Understanding the Organization and Its Context
controls.description: The organization shall determine external and internal issues that are relevant to its purpose and that affect its ability to achieve the intended outcome(s) of its BCMS.
controls.full_text: The organization shall determine external and internal issues that are relevant to its purpose and that affect its ability to achieve the intended outcome(s) of its business continuity management system. These issues shall be taken into account when establishing, implementing, and maintaining the BCMS. The organization shall identify and document these issues and monitor and review information about them.

controls.title: Understanding the Needs and Expectations of Interested Parties
controls.description: The organization shall determine the interested parties that are relevant to the BCMS and their requirements, including applicable legal, regulatory, and contractual obligations.
controls.full_text: The organization shall determine the interested parties that are relevant to the business continuity management system, the requirements of these interested parties (i.e. their needs and expectations), and which of these requirements will be addressed through the BCMS. This includes legal and regulatory requirements, contractual obligations, and the needs of employees, customers, suppliers, shareholders, regulators, and the community.

controls.title: Determining the Scope of the BCMS
controls.description: The organization shall determine the boundaries and applicability of the BCMS to establish its scope, considering internal and external issues, requirements of interested parties, and the organization's mission and objectives.
controls.full_text: The organization shall determine the boundaries and applicability of the business continuity management system to establish its scope. When determining this scope, the organization shall consider the internal and external issues referred to in 4.1, the requirements referred to in 4.2, and the products, services, and activities of the organization. The scope shall be available as documented information.

controls.title: Business Continuity Management System
controls.description: The organization shall establish, implement, maintain, and continually improve a BCMS, including the processes needed and their interactions, in accordance with the requirements of this standard.
controls.full_text: The organization shall establish, implement, maintain and continually improve a business continuity management system, including the processes needed and their interactions, in accordance with the requirements of this document. The BCMS shall be integrated into the organization's overall management processes and structure.
evidence_requirements: Integration evidence with organizational management system

controls.title: Leadership and Commitment
controls.description: Top management shall demonstrate leadership and commitment with respect to the BCMS by ensuring that business continuity policy and objectives are established and are compatible with the strategic direction of the organization.
controls.full_text: Top management shall demonstrate leadership and commitment with respect to the BCMS by ensuring the business continuity policy and business continuity objectives are established and are compatible with the strategic direction of the organization, ensuring the integration of the BCMS requirements into the organization's business processes, ensuring that the resources needed for the BCMS are available, communicating the importance of effective business continuity management and of conforming to the BCMS requirements, ensuring that the BCMS achieves its intended outcome(s), directing and supporting persons to contribute to the effectiveness of the BCMS, promoting continual improvement, and supporting other relevant management roles to demonstrate their leadership as it applies to their areas of responsibility.

controls.title: Business Continuity Policy
controls.description: Top management shall establish a business continuity policy that is appropriate to the purpose of the organization, provides a framework for setting business continuity objectives, and includes a commitment to satisfy applicable requirements and to continual improvement.
controls.full_text: Top management shall establish a business continuity policy that is appropriate to the purpose of the organization, provides a framework for setting business continuity objectives, includes a commitment to satisfy applicable requirements, and includes a commitment to continual improvement of the BCMS. The business continuity policy shall be available as documented information, be communicated within the organization, and be available to interested parties as appropriate.

controls.title: Organizational Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities
controls.description: Top management shall ensure that the responsibilities and authorities for relevant roles are assigned and communicated within the organization, including responsibility for ensuring the BCMS conforms to requirements and reporting on BCMS performance.
controls.full_text: Top management shall ensure that the responsibilities and authorities for relevant roles are assigned and communicated within the organization. Top management shall assign the responsibility and authority for ensuring that the BCMS conforms to the requirements of this document, and reporting on the performance of the BCMS to top management. Roles may include a BC manager, BC coordinator, crisis management team leader, and incident response leads.
evidence_requirements: Appointment letters or terms of reference

controls.title: Actions to Address Risks and Opportunities
controls.description: When planning for the BCMS, the organization shall consider the issues and requirements and determine the risks and opportunities that need to be addressed to ensure the BCMS can achieve its intended outcomes, prevent or reduce undesired effects, and achieve continual improvement.
controls.full_text: When planning for the business continuity management system, the organization shall consider the issues referred to in 4.1 and the requirements referred to in 4.2 and determine the risks and opportunities that need to be addressed to give assurance that the BCMS can achieve its intended outcome(s), prevent or reduce undesired effects, and achieve continual improvement. The organization shall plan actions to address these risks and opportunities, how to integrate and implement the actions into its BCMS processes, and how to evaluate the effectiveness of these actions.

controls.title: Business Continuity Objectives and Plans to Achieve Them
controls.description: The organization shall establish business continuity objectives at relevant functions, levels, and processes. Objectives shall be consistent with the BC policy, measurable, take into account applicable requirements, and be monitored and updated.
controls.full_text: The organization shall establish business continuity objectives at relevant functions, levels and processes. The business continuity objectives shall be consistent with the business continuity policy, be measurable (if practicable), take into account applicable requirements, be monitored, be communicated, and be updated as appropriate. When planning how to achieve its business continuity objectives, the organization shall determine what will be done, what resources will be required, who will be responsible, when it will be completed, and how the results will be evaluated.

controls.title: Planning Changes to the BCMS
controls.description: When the organization determines the need for changes to the BCMS, the changes shall be carried out in a planned and systematic manner.
controls.full_text: When the organization determines the need for changes to the business continuity management system, the changes shall be carried out in a planned manner. The organization shall consider the purpose of the changes and their potential consequences, the integrity of the BCMS, the availability of resources, and the allocation or reallocation of responsibilities and authorities.

controls.title: Resources
controls.description: The organization shall determine and provide the resources needed for the establishment, implementation, maintenance, and continual improvement of the BCMS.
controls.full_text: The organization shall determine and provide the resources needed for the establishment, implementation, maintenance and continual improvement of the BCMS. Resources include human resources, financial resources, technological resources, information resources, and infrastructure needed to maintain the BCMS and execute business continuity plans.

controls.title: Competence
controls.description: The organization shall determine the necessary competence of person(s) doing work under its control that affects the performance and effectiveness of the BCMS, and ensure these persons are competent on the basis of appropriate education, training, or experience.
controls.full_text: The organization shall determine the necessary competence of person(s) doing work under its control that affects the performance and effectiveness of the BCMS, ensure that these persons are competent on the basis of appropriate education, training, or experience, where applicable take actions to acquire the necessary competence and evaluate the effectiveness of the actions taken, and retain appropriate documented information as evidence of competence.

controls.title: Awareness
controls.description: Persons doing work under the organization's control shall be aware of the business continuity policy, their contribution to the effectiveness of the BCMS, the implications of not conforming to BCMS requirements, and their role during a disruptive incident.
controls.full_text: Persons doing work under the organization's control shall be aware of the business continuity policy, their contribution to the effectiveness of the BCMS including the benefits of improved business continuity performance, the implications of not conforming with the BCMS requirements, and their own role and responsibilities before, during, and after a disruption.

controls.title: Communication
controls.description: The organization shall determine the need for internal and external communications relevant to the BCMS, including what, when, with whom, and how to communicate.
controls.full_text: The organization shall determine the need for internal and external communications relevant to the BCMS including on what it will communicate, when to communicate, with whom to communicate, and how to communicate. The organization shall establish, implement and maintain procedures for internal communication among interested parties and employees, receiving and responding to communications from external interested parties, and ensuring the availability of means of communication during a disruptive incident.

controls.title: Documented Information - General
controls.description: The organization's BCMS shall include documented information required by this standard and documented information determined by the organization as being necessary for the effectiveness of the BCMS.
controls.full_text: The organization's business continuity management system shall include documented information required by this document and documented information determined by the organization as being necessary for the effectiveness of the BCMS. The extent of documented information can differ from one organization to another due to the size of the organization, its type of activities, processes, products and services, the complexity of processes and their interactions, and the competence of persons.

controls.title: Creating and Updating Documented Information
controls.description: When creating and updating documented information, the organization shall ensure appropriate identification, description, format, media, and review and approval for suitability and adequacy.
controls.full_text: When creating and updating documented information the organization shall ensure appropriate identification and description (e.g. a title, date, author, or reference number), format (e.g. language, software version, graphics) and media (e.g. paper, electronic), and review and approval for suitability and adequacy.

controls.title: Control of Documented Information
controls.description: Documented information required by the BCMS shall be controlled to ensure it is available and suitable for use where and when it is needed, and it is adequately protected from loss of confidentiality, improper use, or loss of integrity.
controls.full_text: Documented information required by the BCMS and by this document shall be controlled to ensure it is available and suitable for use, where and when it is needed, and it is adequately protected (e.g. from loss of confidentiality, improper use, or loss of integrity). For the control of documented information, the organization shall address distribution, access, retrieval and use, storage and preservation including preservation of legibility, control of changes (e.g. version control), and retention and disposition.
evidence_requirements: Access control matrix for BC documents

controls.title: Operational Planning and Control
controls.description: The organization shall plan, implement, and control the processes needed to meet requirements and to implement the actions determined in Clause 6, by establishing criteria for the processes and implementing control of the processes in accordance with the criteria.
controls.full_text: The organization shall plan, implement and control the processes needed to meet requirements, and to implement the actions determined in Clause 6, by establishing criteria for the processes, implementing control of the processes in accordance with the criteria, and keeping documented information to the extent necessary to have confidence that the processes have been carried out as planned. The organization shall control planned changes and review the consequences of unintended changes, taking action to mitigate any adverse effects, as necessary.

controls.title: Business Impact Analysis - General
controls.description: The organization shall implement and maintain a process for business impact analysis that defines the process for identifying activities supporting products and services, assessing impacts over time of not performing these activities, setting prioritized timeframes for resuming activities, and identifying dependencies and supporting resources.
controls.full_text: The organization shall implement and maintain a formal and documented business impact analysis process. The process shall include identifying activities that support the provision of products and services, assessing the impacts over time of not performing these activities, setting prioritized timeframes for resuming these activities at a specified minimum acceptable level taking into consideration the time within which the impacts of not resuming them would become unacceptable, and identifying dependencies and supporting resources for these activities including suppliers, outsource partners, and other relevant interested parties.

controls.title: Business Impact Analysis - Evaluating Impacts
controls.description: The organization shall identify and document the impact types to be considered, the criteria used for assessing impacts, and the maximum acceptable period of disruption (MTPD/MAO) for each activity.
controls.full_text: Using the business impact analysis, the organization shall identify impact types and criteria relevant to the organization's context, identify activities that support the provision of products and services, assess the impacts over time of disruption to these activities using the identified types and criteria, determine the maximum tolerable period of disruption (MTPD) for each activity by identifying the maximum time period after which an organization's viability would be irreparably threatened, and determine the prioritized timeframes for resuming each activity within its MTPD. These timeframes are known as recovery time objectives (RTO).
evidence_requirements: Completed BIA reports per business unit

controls.title: Business Impact Analysis - Dependencies and Resources
controls.description: The organization shall identify dependencies and supporting resources for prioritized activities, including people, information and data, buildings and associated utilities, facilities and equipment, ICT systems, transportation, finance, and partners and suppliers.
controls.full_text: For each activity within the scope of the BCMS, the organization shall identify dependencies and supporting resources, including people with appropriate skills, knowledge, and competence, information and data (both electronic and paper-based), buildings and associated utilities such as workspace and facilities, facilities, equipment and consumables, information and communication technology (ICT) systems, transportation and logistics, finance, and partners, suppliers, and outsourcers.

controls.title: Risk Assessment - General
controls.description: The organization shall implement and maintain a formal and documented risk assessment process that systematically identifies, analyses, and evaluates the risk of disruptive incidents to the organization.
controls.full_text: The organization shall implement and maintain a formal and documented risk assessment process that systematically identifies, analyses and evaluates the risk of disruption to the organization's prioritized activities and the processes that support them. The risk assessment process shall be consistent with the risk assessment process described in ISO 31000 or an equivalent risk management standard. The organization shall identify risks of disruption to the organization's prioritized activities and their dependencies, systematically analyse identified risks, evaluate which risks require treatment, and determine risks that may require treatment in the context of business continuity.

controls.title: Risk Treatment
controls.description: The organization shall identify and select business continuity risk treatment options consistent with the BCMS objectives, considering the risk appetite and tolerance levels of the organization.
controls.full_text: Based on the outputs of the risk assessment process, the organization shall identify and select appropriate business continuity risk treatment options. Available risk treatment options include risk avoidance, risk reduction, risk sharing (e.g., insurance, outsourcing), and risk acceptance. The treatment options selected shall be consistent with the organization's risk appetite and risk tolerance levels. The organization shall produce and keep documented information about its risk assessment and risk treatment process.

controls.title: Business Continuity Strategies and Solutions - General
controls.description: The organization shall identify and select business continuity strategies and solutions based on the outputs from the BIA and risk assessment to meet recovery requirements and protect prioritized activities.
controls.full_text: Based on the outputs from the business impact analysis and risk assessment, the organization shall identify and select business continuity strategies and solutions that meet the requirements of the business continuity objectives. Strategies and solutions shall be selected that are appropriate for protecting prioritized activities, stabilizing, continuing, resuming, and recovering prioritized activities along with their dependencies, and mitigating, responding to, and managing impacts. Strategies and solutions may include resources needed for their implementation.

controls.title: Resource Requirements for Strategies
controls.description: The organization shall determine the resource requirements to implement the selected strategies and solutions, including people, information, data, physical facilities, equipment, ICT systems, transportation, finance, and third-party services.
controls.full_text: The organization shall determine the resource requirements to implement the selected business continuity strategies and solutions. These resources include but are not limited to people (skills, knowledge, experience, and competence), information and data, buildings and associated physical infrastructure and utilities, facilities, equipment, and consumables, information and communications technology (ICT), transportation and logistics, finance, and partners, suppliers, and outsource providers.

controls.title: Protection and Mitigation Measures
controls.description: The organization shall implement and maintain proactive measures to reduce the likelihood of disruption, shorten the period of disruption, and limit the impact of disruption on key products and services.
controls.full_text: Based on the outputs from the business impact analysis and risk assessment, the organization shall consider and implement proactive measures that reduce the likelihood of disruption, shorten the period of disruption, and limit the impact of disruption on the organization's key products and services. These may include physical protection measures, redundancy provisions, diversification of supply chains, insurance, and technology resilience measures.

controls.title: Business Continuity Plans and Procedures - General
controls.description: The organization shall establish and maintain business continuity plans and procedures to manage disruptions and continue its activities based on objectives identified in the BIA.
controls.full_text: The organization shall establish documented business continuity plans and procedures to manage disruptive incidents. Plans and procedures shall collectively provide the capability to manage the consequences of a disruptive incident taking into account the organization's identified and assessed threats, address the impacts of a disruptive event, be effective in minimizing the consequences through appropriate mitigation strategies, assign roles and responsibilities, be workable and accessible when the location where they are normally housed is inaccessible, and be reviewed, exercised, and updated on a regular basis.

controls.title: Incident Response Structure
controls.description: The organization shall establish an incident response structure that provides a framework for the effective management of incidents, including procedures for activating the business continuity response, managing the incident, and communicating with relevant stakeholders.
controls.full_text: The organization shall have a documented incident response structure and procedures that identify thresholds for activation of the response, assess the nature and extent of a disruptive incident and its potential impact, activate an appropriate business continuity response, have processes and procedures for the activation, operation, coordination and communication of the response, and have the resources available to support the processes and procedures to manage a disruptive incident. Communication procedures shall include details for both internal and external communications.
evidence_requirements: Incident management team structure and contact details

controls.title: Warning and Communication
controls.description: The organization shall establish, implement, and maintain procedures for detecting a potential disruptive incident, monitoring and communicating internally during an incident, receiving and responding to communications from interested parties, and ensuring availability of communication means.
controls.full_text: The organization shall establish, implement, and maintain procedures for detecting a potential disruptive incident and for internal communication within the organization. These procedures shall include monitoring for and detecting incidents and potential incidents, ensuring the availability of the means of communication during a disruptive incident, facilitating structured communication with relevant authorities and ensuring interoperability of multiple responding organizations and personnel, operating and testing communications capabilities intended for use during a disruption, recording vital information about the incident actions taken and decisions made to facilitate post-event review.

controls.title: Business Continuity Plan Content
controls.description: Each business continuity plan shall define the purpose and scope, objectives, activation criteria, roles and responsibilities, communication requirements, specific actions for response, resource requirements, and recovery procedures.
controls.full_text: Each business continuity plan shall define its purpose and scope, objectives of the plan, criteria and procedures for its activation, implementation procedures, roles responsibilities and authorities, communication requirements and procedures, internal and external interdependencies and interactions, required resources, information flow and documentation processes. Business continuity plans shall collectively address recovering prioritized activities within their RTO and at a quality acceptable to the organization, and shall be consistent with the organization's BC policy and objectives.
evidence_requirements: Detailed BC plans with all required elements

controls.title: Recovery and Resumption
controls.description: The organization shall have documented procedures for restoring and returning business activities from the temporary measures adopted during and after an incident to support normal business operations.
controls.full_text: The organization shall have documented procedures to restore and return business activities from the temporary measures adopted during and after an incident. Recovery and return procedures shall include criteria for transitioning from response to recovery, procedures for returning to normal business operations after a disruptive incident, verification that systems and data have been restored to their normal operating state, and procedures for standing down the incident response when the situation has been resolved.
evidence_requirements: Data and system restoration verification procedures

controls.title: Exercise Programme - General
controls.description: The organization shall exercise and test its business continuity procedures to ensure they are consistent with its business continuity objectives and validated to work as intended.
controls.full_text: The organization shall conduct exercises and tests that are consistent with its business continuity objectives, are based on appropriate scenarios that are well planned with clearly defined aims and objectives, collectively validate the entirety of its business continuity arrangements over time, minimize the risk of disruption to operations, produce formalized post-exercise reports that contain outcomes recommendations and actions to implement improvements, are reviewed within the context of promoting continual improvement, and are conducted at planned intervals and when there are significant changes within the organization or to the environment in which it operates.

controls.title: Exercise Types and Scope
controls.description: The organization shall implement various types of exercises including tabletop, simulation, full-scale, and technical recovery exercises to validate all aspects of the business continuity arrangements.
controls.full_text: The organization shall include a variety of exercise types in its exercise programme. These may include tabletop exercises and walkthroughs, simulations, technology-focused exercises (e.g., IT disaster recovery testing), complex exercises that simulate a realistic scenario, exercises involving external parties (e.g., emergency services, suppliers, outsource partners), and full rehearsal/live exercises. The scope and complexity of exercises should evolve over the BCMS maturity lifecycle. Each type of exercise shall have clearly defined objectives and success criteria.

controls.title: Monitoring, Measurement, Analysis, and Evaluation
controls.description: The organization shall determine what needs to be monitored and measured, the methods for monitoring measurement analysis and evaluation to ensure valid results, when monitoring and measuring shall be performed, and when results shall be analysed and evaluated.
controls.full_text: The organization shall determine what needs to be monitored and measured, the methods for monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation as applicable to ensure valid results, when the monitoring and measuring shall be performed, when the results from monitoring and measurement shall be analysed and evaluated, and who shall analyse and evaluate these results. The organization shall retain appropriate documented information as evidence of the results. The organization shall evaluate the BCMS performance and the effectiveness of the BCMS.

controls.title: Evaluation of BC Procedures
controls.description: The organization shall evaluate the business continuity procedures and capabilities to ensure their continuing suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness, especially after exercises or incidents.
controls.full_text: The organization shall conduct evaluations of its business continuity procedures and capabilities to ensure they remain suitable, adequate, and effective. Evaluations shall be conducted at planned intervals, after an exercise or test, after an incident, and when significant changes occur that affect the organization. The evaluation shall assess whether the procedures meet the requirements identified in the BIA and risk assessment, whether recovery objectives (RTO, RPO) can be achieved, and whether plans and procedures are current and accurate.

controls.title: Internal Audit
controls.description: The organization shall conduct internal audits at planned intervals to provide information on whether the BCMS conforms to the organization's own requirements and the requirements of this standard, and is effectively implemented and maintained.
controls.full_text: The organization shall conduct internal audits at planned intervals to provide information on whether the BCMS conforms to the organization's own requirements for its BCMS and the requirements of this document, and is effectively implemented and maintained. The organization shall plan, establish, implement, and maintain an audit programme including frequency, methods, responsibilities, planning requirements, and reporting. The audit programme shall take into consideration the importance of the processes concerned, changes affecting the organization, and the results of previous audits. The organization shall define the audit criteria and scope for each audit, select auditors and conduct audits that ensure objectivity and impartiality, ensure results are reported to relevant management, retain documented information as evidence, take necessary correction and corrective actions without undue delay, and verify actions taken.
evidence_requirements: Audit finding tracker and corrective actions

controls.title: Management Review
controls.description: Top management shall review the organization's BCMS at planned intervals to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness.
controls.full_text: Top management shall review the organization's BCMS at planned intervals to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness. The management review shall include consideration of the status of actions from previous management reviews, changes in external and internal issues relevant to the BCMS, information on the BCMS performance and effectiveness including trends in nonconformities and corrective actions, monitoring and measurement results, audit results, and the fulfilment of business continuity objectives, feedback from interested parties, results of the risk assessment and status of the risk treatment plan, the results of exercises, opportunities for continual improvement, and any changes that could affect the BCMS. The outputs of the management review shall include decisions related to continual improvement opportunities, any need for changes to the BCMS, resource needs, and updated risk treatment plans.

controls.title: Nonconformity and Corrective Action
controls.description: When a nonconformity occurs, the organization shall react to the nonconformity, evaluate the need for action to eliminate causes, implement any action needed, review the effectiveness of corrective action taken, and make changes to the BCMS if necessary.
controls.full_text: When a nonconformity occurs, the organization shall react to the nonconformity and as applicable take action to control and correct it and deal with the consequences, evaluate the need for action to eliminate the cause(s) of the nonconformity in order that it does not recur or occur elsewhere by reviewing and analysing the nonconformity, determining the causes of the nonconformity, and determining if similar nonconformities exist or could potentially occur. The organization shall implement any action needed, review the effectiveness of any corrective action taken, and make changes to the BCMS if necessary. Corrective actions shall be appropriate to the effects of the nonconformities encountered. The organization shall retain documented information as evidence.
evidence_requirements: Corrective action plans and evidence of implementation

controls.title: Post-Exercise Evaluation and Improvement
controls.description: The organization shall evaluate exercises and use the results to identify improvements to the BCMS, update business continuity plans and procedures, and address any gaps or weaknesses identified.
controls.full_text: Following each exercise, the organization shall conduct a formal post-exercise evaluation to capture lessons learned and identify areas for improvement. The evaluation shall assess whether exercise objectives were met, the adequacy of the business continuity plans and procedures, the competence and awareness of participants, the effectiveness of communication and coordination, and any gaps or weaknesses that were identified. The organization shall produce a post-exercise report documenting findings, recommendations, and agreed corrective actions, and shall track these actions to completion.
evidence_requirements: Plan update records based on exercise findings

controls.title: Continual Improvement
controls.description: The organization shall continually improve the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the BCMS through the use of the business continuity policy, objectives, audit results, analysis of monitored events, corrective actions, and management review.
controls.full_text: The organization shall continually improve the suitability, adequacy and effectiveness of the BCMS. The organization shall consider the outputs from analysis and evaluation and the outputs from management review to determine if there are needs or opportunities that shall be addressed as part of continual improvement. Where applicable, the organization shall select appropriate tools and methodologies for investigating the causes of underperformance and supporting continual improvement. These may include trend analysis, benchmarking, maturity models, lessons learned from exercises and incidents, and industry best practices.
